name: test-generator
description: Generate comprehensive test boilerplate following project TDD conventions. Use when creating new tests for modules, classes, or functions. Automatically creates tests with proper structure, type hints, docstrings, and AAA pattern.
capabilities: Generate test files for functions, classes, and modules
Create parametrized test cases with multiple input combinations
Build async test templates with pytest-asyncio markers
Produce pytest fixture templates with proper setup and teardown
Apply AAA pattern and type hints following project conventions
author: Atyantik
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only SKILL.md with usage instructions and 5 Jinja2-style templates for pytest test generation. No executable scripts, network calls, or file writes exist. All static findings are false positives: template variables like {module_name} were misidentified as cryptographic algorithms, markdown code block backticks were misidentified as shell execution, and documentation instructions like 'Read the source file' were misidentified as system reconnaissance.
valueStatement: Writing tests from scratch takes time and requires following project conventions. This skill automatically generates comprehensive test templates with proper AAA pattern, type hints, and parametrized test cases tailored to your project standards.
limitations: Does not execute or run tests directly
Templates require user customization for actual test data
Requires project documentation to be present for full context
Only generates templates, not full implementation

useCases.title: Start new TDD projects
useCases.description: Generate test templates before implementing new functions or classes

useCases.title: Enforce test standards
useCases.description: Create consistent test patterns across team repositories

useCases.title: Build test coverage
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold test files with parametrized and edge case templates

promptTemplates.title: Basic function test
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate test for a simple function
promptTemplates.prompt: generate tests for src/my_project/utils.py - function calculate_total(items: list[float]) -> float

promptTemplates.title: Class with methods
promptTemplates.scenario: Test a class with multiple methods
promptTemplates.prompt: generate tests for UserManager class with create_user and delete_user methods

promptTemplates.title: Async API function
promptTemplates.prompt: generate async tests for fetch_user_data(user_id: str) -> dict function

promptTemplates.title: Parameterized validator
promptTemplates.scenario: Test input validation with multiple cases
promptTemplates.prompt: generate parametrized tests for validate_email function with various valid and invalid email formats

bestPractices: Always run generated tests immediately to confirm they fail (TDD red phase)
Replace all TODO comments with actual test data before implementation
Use parametrized tests for multiple input combinations to maximize coverage
antiPatterns: Avoid mocking internal functions or validators - use real code where possible
Do not write tests that test multiple unrelated behaviors in one function
Avoid over-mocking: only mock HTTP, database, file I/O, time, and random values

faq.question: Which Python versions are supported?
faq.answer: The skill generates tests compatible with Python 3.9+ using modern type hints and annotations.

faq.question: What testing frameworks work with the templates?
faq.answer: Templates are designed for pytest, pytest-asyncio for async tests, and compatible with pytest-cov for coverage.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing test suites?
faq.answer: Templates follow standard pytest conventions and can be added to any tests/ directory structure.

faq.question: Is any data stored or sent externally?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill with no network access, file writes, or external data transmission.

faq.question: Why do generated tests fail initially?
faq.answer: Tests follow TDD methodology. They fail until you implement the actual code being tested. This is expected behavior.

faq.question: How does this compare to other test generators?
faq.answer: Unlike AI-generated tests, these templates follow strict project conventions with AAA pattern, type hints, and docstrings.
